summary:
The most likely diagnosis is a psoas abscess due to the patient’s age-specific presentation including lumbar pain worsening upon extending the right leg, associated mass, and radiographic signs implicating adjacent vertebral tissue; these features align closely with reported characteristics of pediatric cases while ruling out less common differential diagnoses such as pyonephrosis or testicular issues based solely on provided information. \boxed{A}

- Lumbar area lump present  
- Spinal x-ray showing abnormality near vertebrae
Most Probable Diagnosis: Psoas Abscess Given Age & Symptomatology Matching Known Clinical Presentation Without Conflicting Evidence Against This Hypothesis.\boxed{A}
